evidence.snippet: Electric is sparking new opportunities in aviation. Discover how our batteries and battery chargers are reducing costs and lowering aircraft emissions.
We are a leading provider of certifiable electric power systems for high-reliability applications. Our mission: deliver smarter, lighter, and more efficient batteries.

items.notes: Core business is certifiable aerospace propulsion batteries (EPiC family) and associated charging infrastructure for electric aviation including eVTOL, military, and unmanned aircraft.

Current EP Systems customers include NASA, the Federal Aviation Administration, Boeing, Safran, Bell Textron, Diamond Aircraft, VoltAero, Aura Aero and Embraer.
We are a leading provider of certifiable electric power systems for high-reliability applications. Our mission: deliver smarter, lighter, and more efficient batteries.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Sells battery packs, charging hardware, and power management systems to aircraft OEMs including unmanned aircraft programs (Boeing CAV, Aurora Flight Sciences Pegasus cited in press release). Does not manufacture complete drones.

items.name: DC Fast Charger
items.product_type: 200 kW aircraft battery fast charger (in development)

evidence.snippet:
### DC Fast Charging
EP Systems is developing a proprietary 200 kW DC Fast Charger to enable rapid charging and turn around times of electric aircraft.

### Overnight Wall Charger
The 20 kW Wall Charger will allow for slower charging in the hangar, and optimize battery state of charge levels for extended cycle and calendar life of the system.

items.name: Mobile Microgrid Charge Truck
items.product_type: mobile battery charging and second-life energy storage system

evidence.snippet:
### Mobile Microgrid Charge Truck
The Mobile Micro Grid charging solution provides a secondary life for batteries, reducing overall costs and maximizing efficient use of energy and materials. The Micro Grid may be installed as a stationary system directly at airports, or used in a mobile application for a more flexible charging solution.

readiness.notes: EPiC 1.0 achieved FAA TSO-C179b certification in 2024; catalog SKUs with published specs; 500+ designed systems and 15 electric aircraft currently flying. EPiC 2.0 was in late-stage development as of site data.

fetch_requests.reason: Skipped defense page would reveal defense-specific product lines (NAVSEA-certified systems, military UAV/UAS battery variants) to confirm drone supply chain role and product enumeration.
Skipped commercial page would confirm commercial eVTOL and UAV battery product lines and any NDAA/compliance claims for commercial customers.

tagline: Modular lithium-ion propulsion batteries and thermal-containment systems for electric aircraft. FAA TSO-certified EPiC 1.0 (energy and power variants) in production; EPiC 2.0 next-gen variants in development. Also supplies battery management units, charging infrastructure (DC fast and overnight wall chargers), and custom energy systems. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown. Pursuing NAVSEA S9310 certification for military applications.
